sutta: is quite an interesting sutta once while the exalted 1 was staying among the sakians in banyan tree park at kapilavatu maha pajapati came and visited him and after saluting stood at 1 side thus standing she said to the exalted 1 lord well worried that women should be allowed to go forth from the home to homeless life into the dhamma vinaya declared by the tatargata [Music] and the buddha said enough gothamite set not your heart upon the going forth of women from the home to the homeless life into the dhammavinaya declared by the tata gather but mahapadjapati besieged him a second time and a third time in light manner but the exalted 1 replied as before then mahapadjapati the gotham seeing that the exalted 1 would not permit women to go forth from the home sad and sorrowful in tears and sobbing saluted him and departed keeping him on her right just stop here to comment this was actually the master mother of our buddha when our buddha was young according to the legend 7 days after our buddha was born the real mother passed away and then the mother's sister is this mahabhaja party and maha bhajapati was also the wife of the buddha's father in the in the olden days a person could have as many wives as he could afford to keep that so um so in actual fact mahaprabhu was the anti of the buddha and mahabapati had her own son by the name of nanda and as she brought up nanda she also brought up and nursed our buddha just like her own son and when the buddha had become enlightened and came back to see the the sakyans and taught them the dhamma and at that time this mahapadhapati was already very old so she wanted to become a nun and she was the first to ask the buddha for permission to ordain but as you see here the buddha refused her permission now this term continues now the exalted 1 when he had stayed at kapilavatu as long as he desired set out on a journey to visali and in due course going from place to place arrived there and the exalted 1 dwelt near vesali in mahavana at the gabled hall then maha pajapati the gothamine having had her hair cut off and dawn yellow robes set out with a large company of sakian women for vasali and in due course they drew near to the gable hall in mahavana near vesali and maha pajapati stood outside the door her feet swollen and her limbs covered with dust sad and sorrowful sobbing and in tears now the wemble ananda saw her standing down thus with swollen feet and in tears and said to her wherefore gautamin do you stand sad and sorrowful outside the door and she said it is because reverend ananda the exalted 1 will not allow women to go forth from the home to the homeless life into the dhamma vinaya declared by the tata gather and verbal ananda said then wait here go to it until i have asked the exalted 1 to allow women to go forth from the home and the verbal ananda went unto the exalted 1 and saluted and sat down at 1 side so seated he spoke thus lord mahaprabhu stands outside at the door her feet swollen and her limbs covered with dust sad and sorrowful sobbing and in tears saying the exalted 1 will not allow women to go forth lord well worried that women should be allowed to go forth from the home to the homeless light into the dhamma vinaya declared by the tata gata and the buddha said enough ananda set not your heart upon the going forth of women from the home to the homeless life the second and the third time ananda beseeched him in like manner but the exalted 1 gave the same reply then taught the verb the exalted 1 will not allow women to go forth from the home to the homeless life what if i were to ask the exalted 1 in another way and he spoke thus lord if women go forth from the home to the homeless life into the dhamma vinaya and declared by the tata gata can they realize the fruit of stream winning of 1's returning of non-returning and of arahanship [Music] and the buddha said they can [Music] lord if they can since mahaprabhu has been of great service to the exalted 1 for as his aunt nurse and foster mother she gave him milk and the exalted 1's mother died well worried that women should be allowed to go forth and the buddha said if ananda mahaprabhu received these 8 cardinal rules it shall be for her the ordination 1 though she has been ordained a 100 years a nun must pay respect raise her hands in salutation rise up from her seat and salute a monk who has but that day been ordained this is a rule to be honored respected revered venerated and never to be transgressed during her life 2 um none must not spend the rainy season where there is no resident monk this is a rule never to be transgressed 3 twice a month a nun must question the sangha concerning the date of the upusvata day and the next time of preaching this is a rule never to be transgressed for after the reigning seas rainy season a nun must keep the pavarana before both sanghas in respect of 3 matters those seen those heard and those suspected this is a rule never to be transgressed [Music] 5 a none guilty of a serious offense must undergo a penance before both sanghas for the half month this is a rule never to be transgressed 6 after training for 2 rainy seasons in the 6 rules and none must seek full ordination of both sanghas this is a rule never to be transgressed 7 not on any pretext must none revile or abuse among this is a rule never to be transgressed a from today ananda admonition by nuns of monks is forbidden but admonition of nuns by monks is not forbidden this is a rule to be honored respected revered venerated and never to be transgressed during life if maha pajapati received these 8 cardinal rules it shall be for her the ordination now when the verbal ananda had learned these 8 rules from the exalted 1 he went to mahapantapati and told her all the exalted 1 had said and she answered him and said ananda just as a woman or man young and tender in years and fawn of dress would after washing the head received with both hands a garland of lotus flowers of jasmine flowers or of some sweet scented creeper and place it on top of the head even so eyes receive these 8 cardinal rules never to be transgressed all my life then the venable ananda returned to the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated he said lord maha bhajapati the gothamit has received the 8 rules and the buddha said if ananda women had not been allowed to go forth from the home to the homeless life into the dhamma vinaya declared by the tathagata then long would have lasted the holy life for a thousand years would have said dhamma or true dharma would sadama or true dhamma have lasted but now ananda since women have gone forth not for long will the holy life last now and under just for 500 years will the true dharma last just as those clans that have many women and but few men easily fall a prey to robbers and potties even so ananda in whatever dhamma vinaya women are allowed to go forth from the home to the homeless life that holy life will not last long just as when the disease known as white as bones falls upon the field of ripened rice that field does not last long even so ananda in whatever dhamma vinaya women are allowed to go forth that holy life will not last long just as when the disease known as red rust falls upon a field of ripened sugarcane that field does not last long even so ananda in whatever dhamma vinaya women are allowed to go forth that holy life will not last long and now ananda just as a man might build a dive to a great reservoir with a view to the future so that no water could pass beyond it even so i ananda with a view to the future have laid down for nuns these 8 cardinal rules which must not be transgressed so long as they live that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so we see from here after mahamati could not get the permission of the buddha to go forth and then he followed the buddha all the way here walking from kapilava to 2 ways ali and according to the commentary and there was a distance of about 200 miles so that was a pretty long way to walk and [Music] so the venable ananda asked the buddha again for permission for women to go forth and 3 times the buddha refused him and then after that he thought of another way to get rounded now and reminded the the buddha that the buddha owed very much to mahaprabhu who brought her up and gave her milk when she was small etc and so the buddha relented and the buddha made 8 rules for nuns the first 1 is that even a nun has been ordained a long time she must be respect to a monk any monk even though he is very newly ordained then second rule is that monk must not spend the rainy season davasa where there is no resident monk in the theravada tradition every year during the rainy season in india they had the rainy season the winter and the summer seasons are 3 seasons and 3 months out of the 4 months a month or a none must stay in a fixed place and not leave the place more than 6 continuous nights so for a nanna she must spend the vasa in a place where there are monks at least 1 monk and the third condition is that twice a month a nun must ask the bhikkhu sangha for the date of the uposata because i think they have to recite the precepts in front of the monk and then the time of preaching because every 2 weeks they would have to be given a teaching by a senior monk and the fourth rule is after the rainy season a nun must keep the pavarana before both sanghas pavarana is an invitation to criticize it is also practiced by mangala when monks has been the vasa the rainy season and after the 3 months of the rainy season sometimes there is some ill will between monks so they have to each 1 of them they have to in a sangha gathering starting from the most senior manga they have to invite criticism from the other monks invite the monks to criticize them not in case the any of the monks has seen or heard or suspected anything wrong then the monk invites criticism so for a case in the case of a nun she has to invite the nuns to criticize her and later she has to ask the monks to invite the monks to criticize her as well the fifth ruler is she has undergone a serious offense meaning a sangha disease the second most serious offense for a manga nanna is a sangha disease for example to touch somebody of the opposite sex purposely uh out of a lustful desire or to say some words some vulgar words etc so for this class of serious offenses she and none must undergo a penance before both sanghas the monks and the nuns the sixth rule after training for 2 rainy seasons that means for 2 years in the 6 rules a nun she cannot immediately become a nun she has to keep 6 precepts for 2 years that was partly because once there was a nun who came to ordain and after she was ordained then she became pregnant and that was not because she did anything wrong but because she was married before she ordained so after she ordained she conceived and then people talked bad about her so this was probably the main reason why the buddha made a ruler that none must spend 2 years before she gets the full ordination and then the seventh rule is that a nun must not revile or abuse among and the eighth rule is that monks can criticize nuns but nuns are not allowed to criticize monks now sometimes women are not happy with this kind of discrimination against women but it's partly because women are more emotional than men so that if they are kind then they are kinder than men but if they are angry or hateful then they become much more hateful than men so that's for this reason and um towards the end the buddha told ananda that if women had not been allowed to go forth and then the true dharma would have lasted 1 000 years but because women had gone forth then the true dharma would now last only 500 years and so we can see that the the dhamma was unadulterated for 500 years and after 500 years after after the buddhas passing away from that time onwards new books were written new books that contradicted the earlier discourses of the buddha so that if we study the sutasa we find the earliest 4 nikayas the diga nikaya majima nikaya sangyuta nikaya and anguttara nikaya these are the earliest discourses of the buddha these if you read the sutasa there are no contradictions generally but if you read later books and then you find that there's control over the books and then the new newer books they contradict each other as well so we have to be very careful now that the dhamma has become polluted it doesn't mean that the the true dharma is lost okay the true dharma is still here but it is um mixed up with a lot of our dhamma what is contrary to dhamma so it is very difficult for people to distinguish between the real dhamma and the fake dhamma and so [Music] if you don't get the proper guidance because most people don't have the time to study all the books to find out then you might get some wrong views so you see and the reason the buddha at first refused permission for women to go forth was actually to protect the dhamma he wanted the dhamma to pro to last longer to benefit more people and he said once women have gone forth then the sasana the the buddhist religion has become weakened the teachings have become weakened and the buddha gave an example those clans and they have many women but few men easy for easily fall prey to robbers and pot thieves so in the same way [Music] once you have women in the holy life and then the sasana is weakened

items: even a nun has been ordained a long time she must be respect to a monk
a nun must not spend the rainy season where there is no resident monk
twice a month a nun must question the sangha concerning the date of the uposvata day and the next time of preaching
after the rainy season a nun must keep the pavarana before both sanghas
a none guilty of a serious offense must undergo a penance before both sanghas for the half month
after training for 2 rainy seasons in the 6 rules a nun she cannot immediately become a nun
a nun must not revile or abuse among
monks can criticize nuns but nuns are not allowed to criticize monks
